| 36331 : Track Substrate Location for SECS/GEM Tool (Substrate Tracking) | Microsoft.VisualStudio.Services.WebApi.IdentityRef | 3/9/2021 10:07:21 PM | 93 | PFEvatec |  |  |  |  |  |  | PFEvatec | Microsoft.VisualStudio.Services.WebApi.IdentityRef |  | Microsoft.VisualStudio.Services.WebApi.IdentityRef | 4/15/2021 3:32:30 PM |  |  |  |  | Not Automated |  |  |  | Microsoft.VisualStudio.Services.WebApi.IdentityRef | 4/15/2021 3:32:30 PM |  |  | 0 | Microsoft.VisualStudio.Services.WebApi.IdentityRef | 3/9/2021 10:07:21 PM |  |  |  |  | 65625 |  | 4158 | PFEvatec | Phase 2 | R3 |  |  |  |  | PFEvatec\Phase 2\R3 |  | PFEvatec |  |  | The 'PF' web application and 'PSI' server have been launched.The 'PTO' tool is launched.A 'SECS/GEM' tool has been created in the system, with load ports (can be either of type 'FOUP' or cassette for this test).The 'SECS/GEM' tool is online and communicating with 'PTO'.A user has signed into the application with the 'ToolOverview_View', 'ToolDetails_View', and 'Tool Process' security rights.The user has navigated to and is currently located on the 'Tools' overview page of the application. | 2 | New |  |  |  | No | 14 |  | Ready for Review |  | 1/1/9999 12:00:00 AM | Design | 3/9/2021 10:07:21 PM | [1] Verify preconditions. [2] In the 'Tool' overview page, select a 'SECS/GEM' tool which implements an 'E90' standard and has modules configured in the 'PF' application. Click the 'Details' button. [3] In the 'Details' page for the tool, ensure the load port has a cassette or a 'FOUP' that has unknown substrates (i.e there is no track in for the load port and slot the substrate came from). [4] In 'PTO', load the carrier into the load port, assign recipes, and start a process job. [5] In the 'Details', page  ensure the substrates are displayed in the following format:   “Unknown-{Load Port}.{Slot}” (i.e. “Unknown-LP1.01” or “Unknown-LLB.20”). [6] Ensure the substrates are currently of the status: 'Processing'. [7] In the 'Details' page. Examine the 'Substrate' column in the 'Module' section. [8] Navigate to the 'Tool > History' page. Ensure the 'Last known substrate' column is updated with the unknown substrate location. [9] In 'PFDB', ensure that the time the unknown substrate is created will be tracked in the database (in the 'Unknown Substrates' table). | [1]  [2] The 'Details' page is displayed. [3] The load port has a cassette or a 'FOUP' that has unknown substrates.  [4] The process job is started in 'PTO'. [5] The unknown substrate are displayed in the specified format. [6] The substrates are currently of the status: 'Processing'. [7] The value for the 'last known substrate' for the modules is updated with the unknown substrate location during processing (with the specified format). [8] The 'Last known substrate' column is updated with the unknown substrate location in the 'Tool > History' page. [9] The time the unknown substrate is created will be tracked in the database (in the 'Unknown Substrates' table). | R3 | PFEvatec | Verify that when a substrate is unknown, 'PF' will create an unknown substrate name and allow them to be tracked | 733018 | Test Case |
| 36331 : Track Substrate Location for SECS/GEM Tool (Substrate Tracking) | Microsoft.VisualStudio.Services.WebApi.IdentityRef | 3/8/2021 4:52:18 PM | 93 | PFEvatec |  |  |  |  |  |  | PFEvatec | Microsoft.VisualStudio.Services.WebApi.IdentityRef |  | Microsoft.VisualStudio.Services.WebApi.IdentityRef | 4/15/2021 3:45:30 PM |  |  |  |  | Not Automated |  |  |  | Microsoft.VisualStudio.Services.WebApi.IdentityRef | 4/15/2021 3:45:30 PM |  |  | 0 | Microsoft.VisualStudio.Services.WebApi.IdentityRef | 3/8/2021 4:52:18 PM |  |  |  |  | 65494 |  | 4158 | PFEvatec | Phase 2 | R3 |  |  |  |  | PFEvatec\Phase 2\R3 |  | PFEvatec |  |  | The 'PF' web application and 'PSI' server have been launched.The 'PTO' tool is launched.A 'SECS/GEM' tool has been created in the system, with load ports (can be either of type 'FOUP' or cassette for this test).The 'SECS/GEM' tool is online and communicating with 'PTO'.A user has signed into the application with the 'ToolOverview_View', 'ToolDetails_View', and 'Tool Process' security rights.The user has navigated to and is currently located on the 'Tools' overview page of the application. | 2 | New |  |  |  | No | 14 |  | Ready for Review |  | 1/1/9999 12:00:00 AM | Design | 3/8/2021 4:52:18 PM | [1] Verify preconditions. [2] In the 'Tool' overview page, select a 'SECS/GEM' tool which implements an 'E90' standard and has modules configured in the 'PF' application. Click the 'Details' button. [3] Ensure that an E90 location is associated with one of the modules for the tool. [4] Navigate to the 'Tool > Details' page. Click the 'Track In' button. [5] In the 'Track In' page for the tool, ensure the load port has a cassette or a 'FOUP' that has substrates tracked into the 'E90 - SECS/GEM' tool. [6] Navigate back to the 'Details' page. Examine the 'Last known substrate' column in the 'Module' section. [7] Navigate to the 'Tool > History' page. Ensure the 'Last known substrate' column is updated with the substrate location. [8] Ad Hoc: Ensure this information is in sync with the information displayed in the 'PTO' tool. | [1]  [2] The 'Details' page is displayed for the tool. [3] An E90 location is associated with one of the modules for the tool. [4] The 'Track In' page is displayed for the tool. [5] The load port has a cassette or a 'FOUP' that has substrates tracked into the 'E90 - SECS/GEM' tool. [6] The value for the 'Last known substrate' for the modules is updated depending on where substrate's location is during processing. [7] The 'Last known substrate' column is updated with the substrate location in the 'Tool > History' page. [8] | R3 | PFEvatec | Verify that substrate locations can be tracked by 'PF' for 'SECS/GEM' tools | 733035 | Test Case |

[2] Sign in to the application as a user with full 'Security' permissions. Navigate to the 'Transfer Substrates' screen. Begin a transfer process. Do not save changes. [3] In another session (or on another VM), sign into the application as another user with full 'Security' permissions. Navigate to the 'Security' pages. Ensure the other user (in transfer process) is visible in the 'Users' data grid. [4] In the 'Users' data grid, select the user which is currently engaged in the 'Transfer' process. Click the 'Remove' button.  [5] Remove the user. [6] Switch back to the other session where the 'Transfer' is in progress. Attempt to save the changes made during 'Transfer'. The user who has been modified is signed out and redirected to the 'Home Page' (Note: need to confirm behavior of this step).  [7] The user has been removed from the system and cannot log back into the application. | R1 | PFEvatec | 'Remove User': Remove a user who is currently in the process of transferring a substrate | 725956 | Test Case |

[2] In the PF web app, navigate to Upload > Multiple Substrates screen. [3] Select substrates:                                                                                                                            • If track-in is not required, select from multiple boxes.                                                             • If track-in is required, select from tracked-in substrates. Substrate list is populated based on tool configuration. [4] Select a Module:                                                                                                                            • If only one module is docked or substrates are tracked into a module, it is auto-selected.                                                                                                                                        • Otherwise, user selects from available docked modules.                                                                                                                                     [5] Select one or more Files to upload. [6] Optionally add a Comment for each file. [7] Enter a Timestamp:                                                                                                                       • If tool is Process and substrates are tracked into a module, timestamp must be between module dock time and current time.                                                                             • Otherwise, any past time up to current time is valid. [8] Click Upload. [9] PF checks AP configuration. [10] PF updates substrate states:                                                                                                       • If tool is Process and substrate state is New or Pre-Measured → set to Processed     • If tool is Measurement and substrate state is New → set to Pre-Measured [11] If tool requires track-in, PF returns substrates to their original box and slot. | [1]  [2]  [3]  [4] Module is selected and validated. [5] Files are added to the upload list. [6] Comments are saved per file. [7] Timestamp is validated and accepted. [8] PF associates each file with each substrate and selected module. [9] AP requests are created and submitted to EDP for each substrate. [10] Substrate states are updated accordingly. [11] Substrates are returned successfully. | R10.0.8 | PFEvatec | 'Upload File for Multiple Substrates': Verify updates with relation to 'Store files/manual data against modules' | 2862495 | Test Case |

[3] Compare functional specification document for the details page for a file tool that requires 'Track In'. [4] Examine the 'Tool Details' page for the file tool that requires track in. [7] Ensure the user can manage the list of files. [8] Examine the timestamps on the available files.  [9] Complete the 'Upload' wizard process. [4] The user can select from a list of tracked in substrates for upload. [5] The substrates are automatically selected (note: confirm if this feature is implemented in final version, only in func spec). [6] The 'Upload' wizard is displayed. [7] They can manage this list of files (add others or remove existing ones). [8] PF will default the timestamp to the current date and time, but the user can change that to be anytime in the past. [9] Once the files are selected to upload, PF Evatec will associate each file to each of the substrates. [10] PF Evatec will attempt to return the substrates to their source box and slot (note: see 'Return to Source'). | R6.5 | PFEvatec | Verify the process of uploading files for Multiple Substrates for a file tool that supports 'Track In' | 1947393 | Test Case |

| R4 - Smoke Test | Microsoft.VisualStudio.Services.WebApi.IdentityRef | 2/2/2021 10:33:31 PM | 2521 | PFEvatec | WF01 (Tool Configuration) | Tools | File |  |  |  | PFEvatec\WF01 (Tool Configuration)\Tools\File | Microsoft.VisualStudio.Services.WebApi.IdentityRef |  | Microsoft.VisualStudio.Services.WebApi.IdentityRef | 3/3/2026 4:39:28 PM |  |  |  |  | Not Automated |  |  |  | Microsoft.VisualStudio.Services.WebApi.IdentityRef | 3/3/2026 4:39:28 PM |  |  | 4 | Microsoft.VisualStudio.Services.WebApi.IdentityRef | 2/2/2021 10:33:31 PM |  |  | Blocking issue  - 3/3/2026 - Will create task for this  Issue 2 – File Upload Failure (FTM File Archival)FTM archival begins normally, creates a package, and moves the file to the archive folder. The system then repeatedly attempts to store file metadata, but all attempts fail due to the SQL error:  FilesDataFile00060_00071 has no files assigned to it. This triggers repeated store attempts, followed by transaction rollback and package archival failure. Root CauseServer Vision’s database includes the filegroup FilesDataFile00060_00071, but no physical data file has been configured. This is a database infrastructure configuration issue, not an application issue. Required ActionDatabase administrator must add a data file to the filegroup, create the missing file, or reassign storage to a valid filegroup. Action Items Create a task to fix Server Vision database filegroup configuration.DBA/infrastructure to add or configure required filegroup data files.QA to rerun smoke tests after environment is corrected. |  | 43021 |  | 2485 | PFEvatec | Phase 2 | R2 |  |  |  |  | PFEvatec\Phase 2\R2 |  | File |  |  | The 'PF' web application and server have been launched.In 'PTC', 'PF' is selected as the default project.The 'File Tool Manager' has been installed and configured.Ensure the 'File Tool Manager' has been configured to automatically detect files.A new 'File Tool Configuration' has been created and saved.For the tool configuration, a valid directory in the local system has been selected as the file location.Tool sample files have been placed in a directory that will be configured to be monitored by the 'File Tool Manager'.The 'File Tool Manager' has been launched and is communicating the 'PF' server.Ensure the tester is utilizing a valid sample tool (i.e. WOO). | 2 | Completed |  |  |  | No | 32 |  | Change - No Re-review | Microsoft.VisualStudio.Services.WebApi.IdentityRef | 1/1/9999 12:00:00 AM | Ready | 3/12/2021 2:14:35 AM | [1] Verify preconditions and see comments for full details. [3] Ensure when the conditions for the automatic track-in are not met, 'PF' will follow the 'Wafer ID Integrity Violation' process. | [1]  [2] PF will not automatically track in the FOUP containing the substrate with the matching wafer ID. [3] PF creates an unknown substrate with the wafer ID and raise a ‘red flag’ alert for the tool ('Wafer ID Integrity Violation' process). | R3 | PFEvatec | Verify that when an 'Automatic Track In' is not valid, 'PF' will follow 'Wafer ID' integrity violation process for a tool with a 'FOUP' load port type | 725712 | Test Case |

[2] In the 'Track In' page, select a source a box that has a wafer ID set that does not match the wafer ID of a substrate in the intended load port. [3] Track in the substrate (with the set wafer ID) to a tool's load port (via the 'cassette' or 'FOUP' method).  [4] Ensure the specified substrate is read by a 'SECS/GEM' tool ('UnknownSubstrate table) [5] Confirm that all the data collected will be associated to the newly generated unknown substrate (note: determine where to confirm this, history page?). | [1]  [2] The specified box is selected. [3] The substrate is tracked in. [4] The substrate does not match the wafer ID read by the 'SECS/GEM' tool. PF creates an unknown substrate with the wafer ID. [5] All the data collected will be associated to the newly generated unknown substrate. | R3 | PFEvatec | Verify that all data collected will be associated to the unknown substrate when a wafer ID for a substrates not match the wafer ID read by a 'SECS/GEM' tool | 738018 | Test Case |

[2] Navigate to Boxes > Create New Box. [3] Choose method to define substrate type:                                                                            a) Scan Vendor QR code		 b) Search for Box BPS number                                                                                                      c) Define manually  [4] Enter a valid Box Name (e.g., MyBox-01). [5] Select a Work Order from the dropdown. [6] Select a Location from predefined list. [7] Optionally enter Shelf # and Comment. [8] Define substrate slots and types (based on method). [9] Click Save. [10] Confirm PF assigns:                                                                                                                      • Unique Box # with prefix “B”		  • Entry Date = today		  • Reminder Date = today + 6 months		  • Substrate IDs = <BoxName>.<Slot>		  • Initial side = A		  • Status = New | [1]  [2] Box creation screen loads successfully. [3] PF loads appropriate UI based on selected method. [4] Name is accepted (1–12 alphanumeric or dash, not matching ECL ID regex). [5] Only work orders with IsClosed = false are shown. | Ticket # - 16061 - Target: "Storage Location" vs. "Current Location" | Microsoft.VisualStudio.Services.WebApi.IdentityRef | 1/27/2022 4:34:04 PM | 2516 | PFEvatec | WF06 (Logbook) |  |  |  |  |  | PFEvatec\WF06 (Logbook) | Microsoft.VisualStudio.Services.WebApi.IdentityRef |  | Microsoft.VisualStudio.Services.WebApi.IdentityRef | 1/28/2022 9:29:51 PM |  |  |  |  | Not Automated |  |  |  | Microsoft.VisualStudio.Services.WebApi.IdentityRef | 1/28/2022 9:29:51 PM |  |  | 0 | Microsoft.VisualStudio.Services.WebApi.IdentityRef | 1/27/2022 4:34:04 PM |  |  |  |  | 169740 |  | 8905 | PFEvatec | Phase 2 | R6 | R6.3 |  |  |  | PFEvatec\Phase 2\R6\R6.3 |  | WF06 (Logbook) |  | 169517 | https://ops1.peergroup.com/DefaultCollection/PFEvatec/_workitems/edit/169517'Even if a target is installed in a tool it would be helpful to know / see / edit its "location" in the ECL storage. => rename "Location" to "Storage Location" (always displayed & always editable) -> add & display "Current Location" (e.g. directly underneath "Storage Locatation") ONLY IF target is currently installed in a tool (Note: NOT editable)'. | 2 | New |  |  |  | No | 6 |  | Ready for Review |  | 1/1/9999 12:00:00 AM | Design | 1/27/2022 4:34:04 PM | [1] Examine a Target's details. [2] Ensure "Location" has been renamed to "Storage Location" (ensure it always displayed & always editable). [3] Install a target in a tool. [4] Examine the Target's details again. [2] In the 'Windows File Explorer', navigate to the location of the directory chosen for the directory to detect files. Ensure no files have been added there. [3] Add a new file to the directory. Adjust the file name to contain a 'Substrate ID' in the following format: A\|B as a suffix to the substrate ID (i.e. B0001.01A). [4] Perform a transfer and a split to substrates.  [5] Add another file that has a file name that indicates a split substrates using a suffix n the parent Substrate ID or the full Substrate ID (i.e. Box-01.01B.p01 or Box-01_01_p01A).  [6] Start the 'File Tool Manager'.  [7] Start the 'PF' server.  [8] In the 'File Tool Manager', confirm the new files are detected and placed in the 'pending' file view. [9] Confirm that the side has been properly parsed: goto ProgramData for FTM and look at the Index.dat file, in there you can find the name of the file you are looking for and look at the uploadPath - it should have [substrateid]@[side]@[YYYYMMddHHmmss] as the format IF the side was in the original file name (otherwise it will be [substrateid]@[YYYYMMddHHmmss]). | [1]  [2] No files are located in the directory. The hardware configuration screen will display the associated hardware kit for the category.  [3] The selected hardware kit is highlighted. [4] Serial numbers are entered for the required roles. [5] Select is enabled if a different hardware kit is selected and all roles with 'serial # required' have a serial # value. [6] The modal is closed. The user is redirected to the hardware configuration screen. [7] The 'Hardware Configuration' page will display both the current and changed values for both the kit and all of its roles.  [8] The 'Select' change is performed for the specified hardware kit. [4] Ensure in the 'Select Stages' section, the user can select the stage or sub-stage to access the metrology tool or process tool module. [5] Ensure this selection will load and display all of the available parameters for all of the substrates which can be added to the parameter list.   [6] Verify that the 'Substrate Information' is displayed above the stages in the 'Select Stage' section, and when selected displays all the substrate information for the collection. I.E:  [7] Ensure a user can redirect back to 'Stage Matching' screen with no changes saved. | [1]  [2] The 'Select Parameters' dialogue matches the UI functional specification document. [3] The stages and sub-stages from the 'Match Stages' dialogue are displayed in the 'Select Stages' section in an expandable list. [4] The user can select the stage or sub-stage to access the metrology tool or process tool module. [5] All of the available parameters for all of the substrates which can be added to the parameter list are loaded and displayed. [6] The 'Substrate Information' stage performs as specified. [7] A user can redirect back to 'Stage Matching' screen with no changes saved. | R4 | PFEvatec | Access and validate the 'Select Parameters' dialogue screen | 774735 | Test Case |

[2] In the 'Show Collections' card, click the 'New' button. [3] Enter a name of the collection must specified with a maximum of 50 characters. [4] Enter a description that is a maximum of 250 characters. [5] Add (or remove) new substrates to the collection from the 'Substrates' data grid. [6] Add tags to the substrate collection (ensure the color coding is correct). [7] Ensure the collection must have at least one substrate to be saved. [8] Change the default setting of the substrate collection from private to public. [9] The 'Save' button will be enabled when all rules are valid, all required fields are meet and at least one substrates has been added.   [10] Click the save button. [11] Ensure that after the collection is set to public, all users can view, edit and use the collection. [12] Ad Hoc: Validate the entry for all input fields in the 'Substrate Collection'. Ensure duplicate names cannot be added. Ensure all required fields need to be met. Validate the input for the tags. | [1]  [2] The 'New Substrate Collection' panel is displayed in the 'Substrates' screen. [3] The name is validated. [4] The description is validated. [5] The specified substrates are added. [6] The tags are added and are valid. [7] The collection must have at least one substrate to be saved. [8] The substrate collection is changed to public. [9] The 'Save' button is enabled. [10] After saving the panel will update to ‘Edit Collection’. [11] All users can view, edit and use the public collection. [2] In the 'Tool > Details', ensure the 'SECS/GEM' tool selected has a 'FOUP' tracked into a load port with unknown substrates,  [3] Ensure a 'red flag' alert is displayed for the tool and load port. [4] Ensure processing and measurement are currently in progress for the tool. [5] Ensure the unknown substrates associated with the tool's load port do not have data being collected during processing. [6] Ensure when the tool reports that the 'FOUP' has been removed from the load port, 'PF' will automatically remove the unknown substrates from the system. [7] Ensure 'PF' will then clear any associated ‘red flag’ alerts with the removed unknown substrates. [8] Ad Hoc: Ensure this test works for all scenarios where unknown substrates processed. | [1]  [2] The 'SECS/GEM' tool selected has a 'FOUP' tracked into a load port with unknown substrates,  [3] A 'red flag' alert is displayed for the tool and load port. [4] Processing and measurement are currently in progress for the tool. [5] The unknown substrates associated with the tool's load port do not have data being collected. [6] 'PF' will automatically remove the unknown substrates from the system. [7] 'PF' will then clear any associated ‘red flag’ alerts with the removed unknown substrates. [8] | R3 | PFEvatec | Verify that 'PF' will remove unknown substrates from the system that do not have any data collected for 'FOUP' type tool | 725718 | Test Case |

[5] In 'PFDB', query 'ProcessMeasurementView' (see parent requirement for more details on the query). | [1]  [2] The operation mode is changed for different processing modules. [3] The process job is completed. [4] The specified status is updated in 'PF' while the process job is running. [5] Multiple types of 'E10' alarm states can be stored by 'PF'. | R6 | PFEvatec | Confirm multiple types of 'E10' alarm states can be stored by 'PF' | 854543 | Test Case |

[3] The status of the tool corresponds between the screens. Disconnect the tool. [4] Confirm that the status of the tool is now displayed as 'Offline'. [5] Ad Hoc: Disconnect the tool in the 'Tool Configuration > Edit' screen. Confirm the same status is display there as well based on host connectivity. | [1]  [2] The host is connected. [3] The tool is disconnected.  [4] The status of the tool is displayed as specified. [5] | R3 | PFEvatec | Verify that the host connectivity status is provided after disconnecting a 'SECS/GEM' based tool | 725678 | Test Case |
